ddls:
CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "user_pass" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user */,
  "is_admin" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the user has admin privileges */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user account was created */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the user */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the user */,
  "profile_picture" TEXT /* URL of the user's profile picture */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user's account (e.g., active, suspended) */,
  "last_password_change" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last password change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "password_resets" (
  "reset_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each password reset request */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user requesting the reset */,
  "reset_token" TEXT /* Unique token generated for password reset */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the reset request was created */,
  "expires_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the reset request expires */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the reset request was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used to make the reset request */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_password_resets_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "login_attempts" (
  "attempt_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each login attempt */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user attempting to log in */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the login attempt */,
  "successful" INTEGER /* Indicator if the login attempt was successful */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the login attempt was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used to make the login attempt */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location from which the login attempt was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("attempt_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_login_attempts_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., Admin, User) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role's permissions */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* JSON object of permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user-role mapping */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user assigned to the role */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role assigned to the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user was assigned the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "two_factor_authentication" (
  "two_factor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for two-factor authentication settings */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who has two-factor authentication enabled */,
  "enabled" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if two-factor authentication is enabled */,
  "secret_key" TEXT /* Secret key for generating authentication codes */,
  "last_2fa_attempt" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last two-factor authentication attempt */,
  "2fa_type" TEXT /* Type of two-factor authentication method used (e.g., SMS, email, app) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("two_factor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_two_factor_authentication_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "external_identity_providers" (
  "provider_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each provider entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user linked to the provider */,
  "provider_name" TEXT /* Name of the external identity provider (e.g., Google, Facebook) */,
  "provider_user_id" TEXT /* User ID from the external provider */,
  "provider_email" TEXT /* Email associated with the external identity provider */,
  "provider_phone" TEXT /* Phone number associated with the external identity provider */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("provider_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_external_identity_providers_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_sessions" (
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user session */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user associated with the session */,
  "session_token" TEXT /* Unique session token */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the session was created */,
  "expires_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the session expires */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the session was created */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used to create the session */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location from which the session was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_sessions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_logs" (
  "audit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user whose account was affected */,
  "action" TEXT /* Description of the action taken (e.g., 'Password Changed') */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the action was performed */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the action was performed */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used to perform the action */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location from which the action was performed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("audit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "settings" (
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each setting */,
  "setting_name" TEXT /* Name of the setting (e.g., 'Max Login Attempts') */,
  "setting_value" TEXT /* Value of the setting (e.g., '5') */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the setting */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user to whom the notification is sent */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message content of the notification */,
  "is_read" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the notification has been read */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the notification was created */,
  "read_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the notification was read */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., security, informational) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
